nodes.label: FaceGuard Offline System
nodes.description: Fully offline face recognition attendance system built for Hackathon 7.0. Runs four-stage biometric pipeline entirely on-device — no cloud inference, no raw image storage — syncs attendance metadata to S3 when connectivity available.
nodes.rationale: Offline-first design ensures operation in network-constrained environments (highway tollbooths, remote NHAI sites). Privacy-first: no raw images stored or transmitted, only embeddings and metadata.

nodes.label: Four-Stage Biometric Gating Pipeline
nodes.description: Sequential pipeline where each stage gates the next. Only frames passing all preceding stages reach embedding and matching. Minimises compute cost per frame at 30fps while maintaining accuracy and anti-spoofing coverage.
nodes.rationale: Staged gating reduces unnecessary inference — expensive models (MobileFaceNet, MiniFASNet) only run after cheap BlazeFace detection confirms a face is present and passes liveness.

nodes.label: Stage 1: BlazeFace Face Detection
nodes.description: BlazeFace model (float16 quantised, 128x128 input) detects face bounding box at 30fps. Source: MediaPipe Model Hub. ~1.9 MB deployed. Latency ~12ms on Pixel 6.

nodes.label: Stage 2: MiniFASNetV2-SE Passive Liveness
nodes.description: MiniFASNetV2-SE model with Squeeze-and-Excitation channel attention and Fourier Transform auxiliary loss. Rejects printed photos, screen replays, masks. Input: 128x128 RGB, pixels/255. Output: 2-class logits [real_score, spoof_score]. Accuracy: 98.20% on CelebA-Spoof (70k+ samples). ROC-AUC: 0.9984. Source format: INT8 ONNX (facenox/face-antispoof-onnx), converted to TFLite. Size: ~0.6-1.8 MB.

nodes.label: Stage 3: Active Liveness Challenge
nodes.description: Randomised 2-challenge active liveness sequence using MediaPipe Face Landmarker + gesture logic. Challenges: blink, smile, turn left/right, nod. Randomised every session to defeat replay attacks. Max 2.5s per challenge.
nodes.rationale: Randomised challenge order makes replay attacks ineffective. Active liveness complements passive MiniFASNet detection for defense in depth.

nodes.label: Stage 4: MobileFaceNet Embedding & Recognition
nodes.description: MobileFaceNet float32 TFLite model trained with ArcFace loss. Input: 112x112. Output: 128-dim L2-normalised embedding. Cosine similarity search against enrolled embeddings. Match threshold: 0.6. Latency: ~45ms. Size: ~1.9 MB.

nodes.label: MediaPipe Face Landmarker Model
nodes.description: MediaPipe Face Landmarker task bundle (~3 MB) used for gesture/landmark detection in active liveness stage. Deployed as face_landmarker.task.

nodes.label: Offline-First Architecture
nodes.description: Zero network calls during recognition or enrollment. All inference runs on-device via react-native-fast-tflite with GPU/DSP delegate. Targets environments where connectivity is unavailable or unreliable.
nodes.rationale: NHAI sites (highway tollbooths, construction zones) may have limited connectivity. Offline operation ensures attendance can always be recorded regardless of network availability.

nodes.label: S3 Sync with Exponential Backoff
nodes.description: Attendance records persisted locally in SQLite synced to S3 when connected. Sync uploads only metadata (name, code, timestamp, confidence, liveness_score, GPS) — never embeddings. Auto-triggers on connectivity via netinfo. Background sync every 15 minutes via react-native-background-fetch. Failed uploads retried with exponential backoff queue persisting across app restarts.
nodes.rationale: Separating local persistence from cloud sync ensures data is never lost during connectivity gaps. Exponential backoff prevents retry storms on intermittent connections.

nodes.label: Privacy-First Architecture
nodes.description: No raw image storage (pixel buffer discarded after inference). Embedding-only persistence (128-dim float vector in SQLite, not face image). SQLite encrypted with AES-256 via react-native-encrypted-storage, key in OS secure keystore (Android Keystore / iOS Secure Enclave). No embeddings transmitted to cloud. HTTPS-only S3 uploads with TLS 1.2+. SQLite WAL mode for data integrity.
nodes.rationale: Biometric data (face images) is sensitive PII. Storing only mathematical embeddings rather than images reduces regulatory risk and limits harm if device is compromised.

nodes.label: Local SQLite Storage (AES-256 Encrypted)
nodes.description: SQLite database encrypted with AES-256 via react-native-encrypted-storage. Encryption key stored in OS secure keystore (Android Keystore / iOS Secure Enclave). WAL mode enabled. Stores: 128-dim embeddings, employee name/code, attendance records (timestamp, confidence, liveness_score, GPS).

nodes.label: CLAHE Preprocessing
nodes.description: Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization applied to camera frames before model inference. Latency ~15ms on Pixel 6. Implemented in src/utils/.

nodes.label: On-Device Model Size Budget
nodes.description: Total model footprint ~7-9 MB, well under 20 MB budget (55%+ headroom). All models run via react-native-fast-tflite with GPU/DSP delegate where available, fallback to CPU.

nodes.label: Employee Enrollment Flow
nodes.description: Enrollment captures face embedding via camera. Duplicate detection: warns if cosine similarity >75% with existing embedding. Stores only 128-dim embedding, employee name, and code. No raw image retained.

nodes.label: React Native 0.74 Framework
nodes.description: Cross-platform mobile framework targeting Android and iOS. App built with React Native 0.74.

nodes.label: react-native-fast-tflite (TFLite Runtime)
nodes.description: TFLite model runtime for React Native. Uses GPU/DSP delegate where available, falls back to CPU. On iOS uses Metal delegate automatically when 'gpu' parameter passed to loadTensorflowModel.

nodes.label: Performance Benchmarks (Pixel 6)
nodes.description: Benchmarks on Pixel 6 (Android 13, warm): BlazeFace 12ms, CLAHE 15ms, MobileFaceNet 45ms, MiniFASNet 65ms, cosine search 500 employees 3ms, total end-to-end ~550ms (target <600ms), cold start 3.2s. Models warmed in parallel during splash screen.

nodes.label: Cosine Similarity Search
nodes.description: Cosine similarity comparison between MobileFaceNet embedding and enrolled embeddings. Match threshold: 0.6. Search over 500 employees takes ~3ms.

nodes.label: FaceGuardOffline Project Structure
nodes.description: Source layout: src/components (UI), src/constants (AppConfig, thresholds, challenges), src/db (SQLite schema/migrations/queries), src/hooks (useCamera, useFaceDetector), src/ml (TFLite model wrappers/pipelines), src/navigation (AppNavigator), src/screens (Home, Enroll, Recognition, AttendanceLog), src/sync (S3 sync, retry queue, background task), src/types (TypeScript defs), src/utils (CLAHE, cosine similarity, helpers).
